hero.title: Your cyber performance partner
hero.description: Let's make cybersecurity a strength for your business. We're here to help with everything from risk management and compliance to training and continuous monitoring, ensuring your growth is always protected.

useCases.title: Our responses tailored to your cyber challenges
useCases.description: Boost your growth with our customized cybersecurity solutions. Whether it's managing a crisis, ensuring regulatory compliance, or enhancing your protections, our expertise will help you overcome every challenge.

values.title: A solid expertise. For more than 40 years.
values.description: A Neverhack-style value sequence that keeps the message simple, direct, and operationally credible.

items.title: Solutions
items.description: Tailored solutions designed and thought out to meet your security needs and perfectly adapt to your organization, regardless of its size.

items.title: Centralized platform
items.description: We created the Hub by NEVERHACK, a unique platform to facilitate your purchases, centralize your cyber tools, train your employees, or protect your data. One platform for all your solutions.

items.title: Expertises
items.description: Our highly qualified and certified experts ensure the highest security standards to meet all your specific needs.
items.detail: Join our team for concrete and varied missions with high added value.

metrics.title: About NEVERHACK
metrics.description: Driven by a passion for protection, NEVERHACK redefines cybersecurity through its expertise and innovation. Discover our journey, our values, and our commitment to security.

messages.text: Hello, I am Arthur Bataille, CEO of NEVERHACK.
Feel free to connect with the global NEVERHACK team, available worldwide to provide you with expert support wherever you are.
You can also discover our offers, browse our use cases, check our career opportunities, or simply explore the map next to it.
Looking forward to seeing you soon.

hero.title: Built for high-trust public systems
hero.description: This placeholder About page mirrors the Neverhack rhythm: oversized introduction, positioning block, values narrative, founder-style story section, global presence, and a final metrics rail.

positioning.title: A public layer with the density of an operating model
positioning.description: The goal is not just to look premium. The goal is to make expertise feel organized, measurable, and easy to navigate under pressure.
positioning.pitch: We design public experiences that behave like controlled environments: cinematic where needed, utility-heavy where trust depends on it.

story.title: Storytelling is strongest when structure does most of the persuasion
story.description: This block stands in for a founder or institutional story, using a strong image panel and a dense text panel in the same way Neverhack stages internal credibility.
story.name: Public System Lead
story.bio: We treat the public surface as part of the operating environment. Navigation, proof, editorial rhythm, and contact architecture all need to feel like one integrated decision system.

values.title: Four values that shape how the public surface behaves
values.description: The About page keeps the same value narrative language as the homepage, but slows it down into longer explanations.

items.title: Controlled pace
items.description: Every route should feel calm, intentional, and sequenced. Big type, clear anchors, and structured reveals should lead the user instead of competing for attention.

items.title: Institutional clarity
items.description: Credibility should come from rhythm, language, and information hierarchy rather than generic superlatives or oversized claims.

items.title: Visible proof
items.description: Metrics, case structures, legal links, and route continuity should always remain close to the surface.

items.title: System continuity
items.description: The homepage and inner pages should feel like one platform, not like separate campaign experiences.

presence.title: Designed to read as an international, enterprise-capable system
presence.description: This section is a placeholder for regional presence and cross-market capability.

items.detail: Product direction, engineering systems, automation design, and multilingual delivery planning.
Enterprise-facing narrative, growth positioning, and route architecture for international audiences.
Campaign-grade presentation, proof packaging, and local-market trust adaptation.

hero.title: Structured service architecture for enterprise trust
hero.description: This page mirrors the Neverhack approach to offers and use cases: large entry hero, chapter rail, sticky service detail, supporting proof blocks, and FAQ.

tracks.title: Governance and cyber strategy
tracks.summary: Strategic diagnosis, operating-model alignment, control mapping, and board-facing visibility.

tracks.title: Identity and access architecture
tracks.summary: Identity posture, privilege design, approvals, lifecycle controls, and operational access trust.

tracks.title: Resilience and incident readiness
tracks.summary: Preparedness for disruption across response, recovery, crisis roles, and continuity structure.

tracks.title: Exposure and third-party control
tracks.summary: External dependencies, attack surface, supplier visibility, and follow-through governance.

proof.title: Every service chapter should feel connected to a bigger operating system
proof.description: A Neverhack-style public service page needs directional framing, modular lists, and visible continuity into adjacent routes.

pillars.title: Three pillars behind the service architecture

items.title: Diagnosis first
items.description: We start from current-state pressure, not from a pre-boxed package.

items.title: System continuity
items.description: Offers, use cases, and proof blocks should reinforce one another instead of living as separate campaigns.

items.title: Decision usefulness
items.description: The service page should help someone decide whether the model fits, not just admire the brand.

faq.title: Questions that help the route feel credible

items.question: What is this page proving?
items.answer: That a service route can behave like a structured operating environment instead of a generic capabilities list.

items.question: Why use a sticky chapter model?
items.answer: Because it slows the reading pace, keeps the user oriented, and makes each service block feel like a deliberate module.

items.question: Is this final copy?
items.answer: No. This is English-first placeholder content designed to stabilize layout, pacing, and interaction before final messaging.

cta.title: Carry the service logic into contact and proof

hero.title: Use cases, campaigns, and structured proof
hero.description: This route adopts a Neverhack-like editorial portfolio language: large hero, utility filters, strong bordered listings, and case rhythm instead of a gallery feel.

grid.title: Proof archive
grid.description: A filtered, editorial list of placeholder cases designed to feel closer to a campaign and use-case archive than a creative studio gallery.

cases.title: Identity operating model

cases.title: Resilience command layer
cases.summary: A placeholder case about continuity posture, incident sequencing, and operational recovery clarity.

cases.title: Third-party risk map
cases.summary: A placeholder case about supplier exposure, critical dependencies, and visible external risk prioritization.

cases.title: Board risk dashboard
cases.summary: A placeholder case about turning cyber noise into executive-facing signals, thresholds, and decision pathways.

hero.title: Move from exploration to a guided conversation
hero.description: The contact page keeps the public rhythm intact: oversized hero, trust signals, structured channels, staged form flow, offices, and legal utility.

scope.title: Scope
scope.detail: Share the route, the audience, the pressure point, and the expected outcome.

reply.title: Reply
reply.detail: The first answer should clarify fit, next steps, and the right shape of conversation.

formBlock.title: A two-step form that feels staged, not generic
